fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: New Jersey Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in New Jersey — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license new jersey, dental assistant certification new jersey, RDA new jersey, DANB CDA new jersey, dental assistant radiography new jersey

board.name: New Jersey State Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address:
New Jersey State Board of Dentistry
124 Halsey Street
P.O. Box 45059
Newark, New Jersey 07101

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in New Jersey.

steps.title: Access the MyLicense Portal
steps.description: Navigate to the MyLicense Online Application System for the New Jersey State Board of Dentistry.

steps.title: Create or Log In
steps.description: If you are a new user, create an account using the 'Register a New User' option. Existing users should log in with their credentials.

steps.title: Initiate Application
steps.description: Select 'Board of Dentistry' and then 'Dental Assistant Radiologic Technologist (DRT)' from the application options to begin your online registration.

steps.title: Complete Application & Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the application form and upload necessary supporting documentation, such as proof of qualifications (e.g., DANB certifications, CODA-accredited program completion, CPR card).

steps.title: Pay Fees
steps.description: Submit the required application fee ($100) and initial registration fee ($75) via the MyLicense portal to complete your submission.

steps.title: Monitor Status
steps.description: Regularly check the MyLicense portal for updates on your application status and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the Board.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the New Jersey State Board of Dentistry by registration.

faqs.question: Does New Jersey require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, New Jersey requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in New Jersey.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in New Jersey?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the New Jersey State Board of Dentistry by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in New Jersey
hero.dek: To register as a Dental Radiologic Technologist (DRT), a specific category of Dental Assistant, in New Jersey, applicants must utilize the MyLicense Online Application System provided by the New Jersey State Board of Dentistry.

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, navigate to the MyLicense portal and click 'Apply for a New License'. Follow the prompts to select 'Board of Dentistry' and 'Dental Assistant Radiologic Technologist (DRT)', then click 'Register a New User' to set up your account credentials before proceeding with the application.
